chapter_context.summary: Recalls the terms of the broken Sinai Covenant, denounces Judah's return to ancestral sins, and uncovers a secret plot by the men of Anathoth against Jeremiah.

outline.text: Solemn Demands and Curses of Broken Sinai Covenant
outline.explanation: This section focuses on Solemn Demands and Curses of Broken Sinai Covenant (11:1-8), highlighting its theological significance and showing how it develops the central message of Jeremiah.

outline.text: Idolatrous Conspiracy in Judah and Green Olive Tree Consumed
outline.explanation: This section focuses on Idolatrous Conspiracy in Judah and Green Olive Tree Consumed (11:9-17), highlighting its theological significance and showing how it develops the central message of Jeremiah.

outline.text: Plot of Men of Anathoth Against Jeremiah Uncovered
outline.explanation: This section focuses on Plot of Men of Anathoth Against Jeremiah Uncovered (11:18-23), highlighting its theological significance and showing how it develops the central message of Jeremiah.

key_themes.title: Prophecy of Warning
key_themes.description: This chapter explores key facets of prophecy of warning, revealing how God's holy character directs both judgment and redemption.

key_themes.title: The Weeping Prophet
key_themes.description: Jeremiah's deep emotional connection to the message and the people, illustrating the heart of God breaking for His children's rebellion.

devotional.question: Reflecting on the warnings and historical accounts of Chapter 11, in what ways does Jeremiah's call to faithfulness encourage you in your own walk today?
How does this chapter prompt you to choose covenant obedience and trust in God's promises of restoration over temporary security?

cultural_historical.title: The Fall of Jerusalem and the Exile
cultural_historical.text: The historical backdrop of Jeremiah's ministry, witnessing the gradual collapse of Judah under the Babylonian Empire and the destruction of the Temple.
